description_long: This object is a vintage wooden television set with a boxy, rectangular shape and a prominent front-facing glass screen. The front panel features a display showing a cartoon, surrounded by a thick wooden bezel, and a set of three rotary control knobs and a speaker grille on the right side. The television stands on four angled legs, giving it a mid-century modern appearance. The sides and top are finished in a wood grain texture, while the back panel is plain with ventilation holes and some small colored markings. The overall construction suggests a solid wood or wood-veneer exterior with metal and glass components for the screen and controls. This type of television would have been common in urban homes or public spaces during the mid-20th century.
description: A vintage wooden television set with a glass screen, rotary knobs, and angled legs, featuring a mid-century modern design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the glass screen displaying a cartoon, three rotary knobs, a speaker grille, and the wood-textured bezel.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the plain wood grain surface with no visible controls or features.
description_view_2: Rear view showing a plain back panel with ventilation holes and small colored markings, lacking any visible ports or controls.
description_view_3: Right side view with a continuous wood grain surface, similar to the left side, and no visible features.
attribute_bezel_design: thick, brown wood, rectangular, matte finish, mid-century style
attribute_control_button_layout: three rotary knobs and a button in a vertical cluster on the front right panel
